data.title: Micron, Nike Set To Report Earnings As Markets Watch Out For Core PCE Price Index
data.description:
Get ahead of the market by subscribing to Seeking Alpha's Wall Street Week Ahead, a preview of key events scheduled for the coming week. The newsletter keeps you informed of the biggest stories set to make headlines, including upcoming IPOs, investor days, earnings reports, and conference presentations.

Wall Street's major averages opened on an indecisive note on Friday, as President Donald Trump will decide about a U.S. strike on Iran within two weeks, while investors weighed the Federal Reserve's rate decision. Trump is expected to decide within two weeks on a possible U.S. strike on Iran amid the latter's conflict with Israel. Investors took some relief from this after talks of the U.S. entering the tensions hurt sentiments. Wall Street on Wednesday erased modest gains to end little changed, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said uncertainty stemming from tariffs continued to call for a "wait-and-see" approach to monetary policy actions. The Fed left its policy rate at 4.25%-4.50% on Wednesday for a fourth straight meeting.

Amidst escalating tensions in the Middle East and investors trying to gauge the latest Fed decision, markets will keep a close eye on economic events scheduled for next week. Monday will include reports on Global Composite PMI, Existing Home Sales, while the House Price Index, and Richmond Fed Manufacturing Index will get published on Tuesday. Reports on MBA Mortgage Applications and New Home Sales are scheduled on Wednesday, while Thursday will see Core PCE Prices, GDP, and Jobless Claims. The week will end with reports on Personal Income, Personal Spending, PCE Price Index, and Core PCE Price Index.

FedEx (FDX), Carnival Corp (CCL), BlackBerry (BB), Micron (MU), Nike (NKE), Walgreens Boots (WBA), Paychex (PAYX), and Daktronics (DAKT) will report earnings next week.

______________________________________________________________________

Earnings spotlight: Monday, June 23: FactSet Research (FDS), Commercial Metals (CMC), See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Tuesday, June 24: FedEx, Carnival Corp, BlackBerry (BB). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Wednesday, June 25: Micron (MU), Paychex, Daktronics. See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Thursday, June 26: Nike, Walgreens Boots (WBA), Concentrix (CNXC). See the full earnings calendar.

Earnings spotlight: Friday, June 27: Apogee (APOG). See the full earnings calendar.

Volatility watch: The conflict between Israel and Iran will continue to be front and center for equity and commodities markets. Nektar Therapeutics (NKTR) and INmune Bio (INMB) have a higher level of implied volatility than normal based on options trading. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Columbus Circle Capital Corp. I (CCCMU), Coinsilium Group (OTCQB:CINGF), and Regencell (RGC). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include PG&E (PCG) and Copart (CPRT).

Dividend watch: Companies that have an ex-dividend next week include FedEx (FDX), Walt Disney Company (DIS), Restaurant Brands (QSR), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), Seagate Technology (STX), Philip Morris (PM), Medtronic (MDT), Humana (HUM), Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP), and British American Tobacco (BTI). Read through some dividend stock picks from Seeking Alpha analysts.

Investor Events: The four-day Samsara (IOT) Beyond event will begin on Monday, which is expected to highlight the company's innovations in safety, efficiency, and sustainability. Meanwhile, the Northland Growth Conference will begin on Tuesday, with participation from FatPipe (FATN), Asure Software (ASUR), Innventure (INV), and TeraWulf (WULF).

Micron Earnings Preview: Micron is scheduled to report its third quarter earnings next week. Analysts expect the company to report earnings per share of $1.59 on a revenue of $8.83B. According to analysts, investors should look for signs of AI demand continuation and look at gross margins closely. Fourth quarter guidance will also be in focus as the AI demand landscape continues to evolve, disrupting cyclical patterns in memory markets.

Nike Earnings Preview: Nike will report its fourth quarter earnings next week, and is expected to report earnings per share of $0.12 on a revenue of $10.7B. Analysts see gross margin headwinds in the year ahead, due to the impact of tariffs, and ongoing inventory clearance activity. However, there is some hope with new management taking over the reins of the company. Nike believes that the worst may nearly be over.

data.title: Light Up Your Retirement With Utility Income
data.description:
Co-authored by Treading Softly.

You know you've become an adult when you spend more of your time turning off lights than you do turning them on.

There's the cliché saying that a father will look at his children who've left their lights on and ask if they are shareholders of the utility company. It's the idea that they're spending their parents' money in a wasteful way – more likely, thoughtlessly – and he wants to know if they're getting some sort of kickback for it.

For many of us, we pay our power bill without a second thought. That's the kind of income that I want to collect! I want money to come in from companies that people pay the bills of without a second hesitation. Not income that's exposed to consumer whims, like high-end shoes, or readily able to be cut off, like your favorite streaming service. Instead, I want essential income – income that comes in from essential sources that you rely on day-to-day. Utilities are one of those key sources of income for me.

Today, I want to look at two different avenues to collect income from utilities and other infrastructure projects you rely on every single day, without even thinking about them.

Let's dive in!

Pick #1: AQN – Yield 4.5%

Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp (AQN) is a regulated multi-utility company. AQN is focused on being primarily a power, water, and natural gas utility provider to jurisdictions all across the United States, as well as in Bermuda and South America. AQN reported its first quarter since it closed on its large asset sales.

AQN is now a simplified company with a regulated services group and a hydro group. Earnings for Q1 were strong, with both segments performing, while interest expense was down: Source.

A significant source of near-term growth for AQN will come from getting authorization to increase prices. Part of being a "regulated" utility is that price increases have to be authorized by the relevant state agency. AQN has made progress with 7 cases that will be implemented by Q2 2025 and has another 7 cases still pending.

In the earnings call, management briefly discussed the potential sale of the Hydro segment, an idea that was suggested by activist investor Starboard. Management was non-committal, noting that they are open to selling it only if it is accretive and doesn't necessarily feel any pressure to sell. So, for now, it is an open question whether AQN will become a pure-play regulated utility investment or whether it is going to retain exposure to the hydro unit as well.

Last week, AQN had an investor update call, and CEO Rod West provided us with his guidance, outlook, and vision for the future of AQN.

A strong theme throughout the call was a "back to the basics" approach, focused on making AQN a premium pure-play regulated utility. West noted that, to be great, they first had to be good.

Guidance for 2025 is for $0.30-$0.32 in adjusted EPS, with an outlook for aggressive growth. AQN projects 16% growth in 2026 and 22% in 2027 at the midpoint of guidance: Source.

If achieved, this pace of growth should be enthusiastically embraced by the market. For income investors, the news is a bit less rosy, as AQN's projections assume the dividend remains fixed at $0.26.

Cash will be retained to help fund ~$2.5 billion in CapEx through 2027 without needing to raise equity.

Management made it clear that maintaining their BBB credit rating is a top priority, and that makes sense. Earnings growth will primarily come from expanding the rate base through a combination of additions and various rate cases they expect will be settled in the coming years.

To a lesser extent, management has stated a goal to reduce G&A as a percentage of revenue from 38% to 31-33%.

Ultimately, we think Wall Street is going to see this as a good plan, and we expect we'll see some more favorable outlooks in the coming months. For our purposes, AQN has a low yield, and guidance is that it won't change for at least the next three years.

Pick #2: UTG – Yield 6.6%

Reaves Utility Income Fund (UTG) is a utility-focused CEF (Closed-End Fund) with a focus on high-quality utility and infrastructure holdings. These holdings are historically lower risk and produce strong and growing dividend cash flows for their holders.

Its top holdings are some of the largest and well-known publicly traded utilities: Source.

Over its extended lifespan, UTG has not only provided strong income to its holders, but also seen its NAV climb:

This is an impressive feat, but also doubles down on the understanding that any harvested capital gains or return of capital that may be paid out from time to time are not part of a destructive pattern towards the fund's NAV but instead are management decisions made over the long run to try and help the NAV stay stable or even grow with time. This means that any dollar invested in UTG, once all the dividends have been removed from the equation, has actually seen growth from the fund's initiation until now. Your shares are worth more, all while paying out fantastic income.

Today, UTG offers an attractive yield along with the future prospects of continued strong NAV performance due to the fund's holdings. It's worthwhile to hold this fund in your retirement portfolio for income and for long-term capital gains.

Conclusion

AQN and UTG both offer us different ways to tap into the utility market. One is a pure-play regulated utility that is unloved and beaten down because of poor choices by prior management, getting into the unregulated space. The other is a well-established and storied fund that provides you with strong and steady income and capital gains.

Up until a couple of years ago, AQN was smashing UTG for its total return, largely because of its excellent growth record, but it got ahead of itself, stumbled, and fell flat on its face. Now, UTG has a better total return over the long run. Yet, both have been able to provide over 200% of total return in their lifetime. This means that even with the stumbles, the falls, and the mistakes, both options were able to more than double your money.

Looking forward to retirement, you want holdings that will provide you with low-risk income but also provide you with the opportunity to see your capital either grow or be preserved. Both of these options allow you to do that and collect great income in the meantime. Now, you can leave the lights on at all your friends' houses, and if they ask you if you're a shareholder, you can proudly say "yes! Thank you for paying your power bill and for my retirement!"

That's the beauty of my Income Method. That's the beauty of income investing.

data.title: Precious Metals Royalty And Streaming Companies - The May Report
data.description:
Precious metals royalty and streaming companies represent a very interesting sub-industry of the precious metals mining industry. They provide some leverage to the growing metals prices, similar to the typical mining companies; however, they are less risky in comparison to them. Their incomes are derived from royalty and streaming agreements. Under a metal streaming agreement, the streaming company provides an upfront payment to acquire the right to future deliveries of a predefined percentage of the metal production of a mining operation.

The streaming company also pays some ongoing payments that are usually well below the market price of the metal. They can be set as a fixed sum (e.g., $300/toz gold) or as a percentage (e.g., 20% of the prevailing gold price), or a combination of both (e.g., the lower of a) $300/toz gold and b) 20% of the prevailing gold price). The royalties usually apply to a small fraction of the mining project production (usually 1-3%), and they are not connected with ongoing payments. They can have various forms, but the most common is a small percentage of the net smelter return ("NSR"). The NSR is calculated as revenues from the sale of the mined products minus transportation and refining costs.

To better track the overall performance of the whole sub-industry, I created a capitalization-weighted index (the Precious Metals Royalty and Streaming Index) consisting of 11 companies (in June 2020, expanded to 15). Later, based on the inquiries of readers, I also introduced an equal-weighted version of the index. Until March 2021, both indices included the same companies and were calculated back to January 2019.

However, some major changes occurred in April 2021. Due to the boom of the royalty and streaming industry and the emergence of many new companies, the indices experienced two major changes. First of all, the market capitalization-weighted index was modified to include only the 5 biggest companies: Franco-Nevada (FNV), Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM), Royal Gold (RGLD), Osisko Gold Royalties (OR), and Sandstorm Gold (SAND). The combined weight of these 5 companies on the old index was around 95%, therefore, the small companies had only a negligible impact on their performance. The values of the index were re-calculated back to January 2019, and between January 2019 and March 2021, the difference in the overall performance of the old and the new index was only 2.29 percentage points. The second change is related to the equally weighted index that was expanded to 20 companies.

The previous editions of the monthly report can be found here: May 2019, June 2019, July 2019, August 2019, September 2019, October 2019, November 2019, December 2019, January 2020, February 2020, March 2020, April 2020, May 2020, June 2020, July 2020, August 2020, September 2020, October 2020, November 2020, December 2020, January 2021, February 2021, March 2021, April 2021, May 2021, June 2021, July 2021, August 2021, September 2021, October 2021, November 2021, December 2021, January 2022, February 2022, March 2022, April 2022, May 2022, June 2022, July 2022, August 2022, September 2022, October 2022, November 2022, December 2022, January 2023, February 2023, March 2023, April 2023, May 2023, June 2023, July 2023, August 2023, September 2023, October 2023, November 2023, December 2023, January 2024, February 2024, March 2024, April 2024, May 2024, June 2024, July 2024, August 2024, September 2024, October 2024, November 2024, December 2024, January 2025, February 2025, March 2025, April 2025.

As of the end of May, Wheaton Precious Metals' market capitalization approached $40 billion. The second-biggest precious metals R&S company, Franco-Nevada, had a market capitalization of slightly over $32.5 billion. At the opposite end of the industry was positioned Star Royalties (OTCQX:STRFF), with a market capitalization of slightly over $10 million. No major changes occurred in the ranking of companies by market capitalization compared to April, with only Silver Crown Royalties (SCRI:CA) outgrowing Star Royalties.

May was another good month for the precious metals R&S companies. Out of 17 companies, 14 recorded share price growth, and 3 experienced losses. The biggest gains were experienced by Empress Royalty (OTCQX:EMPYF). Its share price grew by more than 36%, fueled not only by record-high gold prices but also by positive Q1 financial results. Gold Royalty (GROY) experienced more than 23% gains, supported by similar factors. On the other hand, the biggest losses were recorded by Star Royalties. Its share price declined by more than 18%. The decline was a continuation of the trend initiated in April, when the problems at Green Star Royalties, Star Royalties' partially owned subsidiary, were reported.

In May, the gold price remained almost flat, and the silver price increased slightly. The share price of the SPDR Gold Trust ETF (GLD) declined by 0.06%, and the share price of the iShares Silver Trust ETF (SLV) grew by 1.35%. The precious metals miners did much better, as the VanEck Vectors Gold Miners ETF (GDX) and the VanEck Vectors Junior Gold Miners ETF (GDXJ) grew by 3.35% and 6.38% respectively. Slightly worse numbers experienced the precious metals R&S companies, as the Precious Metals R&S Index grew by 1.04%, and the Precious Metals R&S Equally Weighted Index grew by 5.19%.

The May News

Although the majority of May news was related to the Q1 financial result releases, there were also several deal announcements. The biggest one was Franco-Nevada's $1.05 billion acquisition of the 7.5% Gross Margin Royalty on the Cote gold mine.

Franco-Nevada (FNV) reported Q1 revenues of $364.9 million, operating cash flow of $288.9 million, and net income of $209.8 million. Compared to the previous quarter, the numbers increased by 14.8%, 18.9%, and 19.6% respectively, and all of them reached new all-time highs, fueled by record-high gold prices. Franco-Nevada ended Q1 with cash of $1.128 billion and debt-free. Franco-Nevada also declared a dividend of $0.38 per share. It will be paid on June 26 to shareholders of record as of June 12.

On May 27, Franco-Nevada announced the acquisition of a 7.5% Gross Margin Royalty on the Cote gold mine for $1.05 billion. Franco-Nevada also announced that it negotiated with IAMGOLD (IAG) and Sumitomo (OTCPK:SSUMY), the mine owners, a new royalty agreement with clarified audit and information rights. IAMGOLD (IAG) and Sumitomo (SSUMY) will also be granted an option to repurchase 25% of the royalty at an IRR of SOFR+1.1%, and another 25% at an IRR of 10%. At the current gold price, the royalty should generate revenues of around $70 million per year.

On May 2, Franco-Nevada released its 2025 Asset Handbook and 2025 Sustainability Report.

Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM) reported Q1 revenues of $470.4 million, operating cash flow of $360.8 million, and net income of $254 million. It means a quarterly growth of 23.6%, 12.9%, and 188.3% respectively. Just like Franco-Nevada, Wheaton Precious Metals also reached new record highs at all three above-mentioned financial indicators. The company ended Q1 with cash of $1.086 billion, and almost debt-free. Wheaton reiterated its 2025 guidance of 600,000-670,000 toz of gold equivalent. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.165 per share, which means a 6.5% increase. The payment date was set at June 10, with a record date of May 28.

On May 22, Wheaton released its 2024 Sustainability Report and 2024 Climate Change Report.

Royal Gold (RGLD) announced Q1 revenues of $191.7 million, operating cash flow of $136.4 million, and net income of $113.5 million. What is interesting, while the net income experienced small quarterly growth and reached a new record-high, the revenues and operating cash flow declined slightly, despite the record-high gold prices. Royal Gold ended Q1 with cash of $240.8 million and debt-free. The company also announced a dividend of $0.45 per share, payable on July 17, to shareholders of record as of July 3.

On May 21, Royal Gold announced a $200 million acquisition of a 0.3% NSR royalty and a gold stream on Solaris Resources' (SLSR) Warintza copper-gold-molybdenum project. Under the streaming agreement, Royal Gold will be entitled to 20 toz gold per million lb copper produced. The ongoing payments will equal 20% of the spot gold price until 90,000 toz gold is delivered, and 60% thereafter. Warintza contains resources of more than 30 million lb of copper equivalent, and a PFS is expected in Q3. The production start-up is projected for 2030.

Osisko Gold Royalties (OR) generated revenues of $54.9 million, operating cash flow of $46.1 million, and net income of $25.6 million. The revenues and operating cash flow experienced a quarterly decline, attributable mainly to lower gold equivalent sales. However, the company reiterated its 2025 guidance of 80,000-88,000 toz of gold equivalent. Osisko ended Q1 with cash of $63.1 million and debt of $80 million. The company also raised its dividend by 20%. The next one, amounting to $0.055 per share, will be paid on July 15, to shareholders of record as of June 30.

On May 8, the shareholders approved a name change from Osisko Gold Royalties to OR Royalties.

Triple Flag Precious Metals (TFPM) reported Q1 revenues of $82.2 million, operating cash flow of $65.9 million, and net income of $45.5 million. Compared to the previous quarter, the numbers grew by 10.8%, 3.8%, and 10.2% respectively. All of them reached new record highs. Triple Flag ended Q1 with cash of $22.4 million, and nearly debt-free. The company reiterated its 2025 guidance of 105,000-115,000 toz of gold equivalent.

The quarterly dividend of $0.055 per share will be paid on June 16, to shareholders of record as of May 30.

Sandstorm Gold (SAND) generated revenues of $50.1 million, operating cash flow of $40.7 million, and net income of $10.5 million in Q1. The revenues reached a new record high. During the quarter, 3.1 million shares worth $19.1 million were purchased and cancelled. Sandstorm ended Q1 with cash of $11.7 million, and debt of $340 million. The 2025 production guidance envisions 65,000-80,000 toz of gold equivalent.

Gold Royalty (GROY) reported Q1 revenues of $3.1 million, operating cash flow of $2.5 million, and net income of -$1.2 million. While the revenues declined slightly, the operating cash flow increased, and the net loss decreased when compared to Q4. The company reiterated its 2025 guidance of 5,700-7,000 toz of gold equivalent. As of the end of Q1, it held cash of $3.4 million, and debt of $51.8 million.

Metalla Royalty (MTA) generated revenues of $1.7 million, operating cash flow of $0.6 million, and net income of -$0.7 million. While the revenues experienced a 19% decline compared to the previous quarter, the operating cash flow improved from -$0.4 million to $0.6 million, and the net loss declined from -$1.1 million to -$0.7 million. Metalla ended Q1 with cash of $9 million and debt of $13 million.

On May 28, Metalla provided a portfolio update. The most important news is that the 4% NSR royalty on Polymetals Resources' Endeavor mine should start generating cash flows as soon as this month.

EMX Royalty (EMX) reported Q1 revenues of $8.4 million, operating cash flow of $1.3 million, and net income of $1.3 million. EMX repurchased and cancelled 1,479,792 shares during and initiated a new normal course issuer bid in Q1. The company also reiterated its 2025 guidance of 10,000-12,000 toz of gold equivalent, and adjusted royalty revenues of $26-32 million. As of the end of Q1, the cash on hand amounted to $25.9 million, and total debt amounted to $34.6 million.

On May 13, EMX announced that EMX entered an agreement with Galileo Resources, according to which EMX can earn a 1% NSR royalty over the Ferber project by conducting mapping, sampling, etc., identifying exploration targets, and managing the first phase of exploration drilling on the property. The works will be funded by Galileo, and EMX will receive a 7.5% management fee. Subsequently, EMX will have the option to purchase an additional 0.5% NSR royalty for $1 million.

On May 29, EMX announced that it optioned the Trench gold project to Ridgeline Minerals (OTC:RDGMF). Ridgeline will be able to acquire 100% of Trench gold by paying EMX an execution payment of $250,000, option payments of $650,000, and expending $500,000 on exploration over the next 5 years. Moreover, EMX will retain a 3% NSR royalty. The deal also includes milestone payments of up to $1.75 million.

Vox Royalty (VOXR) announced Q1 revenues of $2.7 million, operating cash flow of $1 million, and net income of -$0.4 million. Despite record-high gold prices, Vox was unable to record a quarterly growth in revenues, and it ended the quarter with a net loss. The good news is that Vox increased its 2025 revenue guidance from $12-14 million to $13-15 million. The reason is the Kanmantoo royalty acquisition. Vox ended Q1 with cash of $9.1 million, and debt-free. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.0125 per share. It will be paid on July 14 to shareholders of record as of June 30.

On May 15, Vox announced the abovementioned acquisition of a 2.5% NSR royalty over the Kanmantoo copper-gold mine for $11.7 million. After 187 million lb is produced (approximately 160 million lb remaining), the royalty will be reduced to 0.5%. The mine should produce 26.5-31 million lb copper this year, along with gold and silver by-products.

On May 22, Vox announced that Northern Star Resources (OTCPK:NESRF) released an updated resource estimate for the Red Hill gold project, where Vox holds a 4% Gross Revenue Royalty over mining lease M27/57.

On May 27, Vox announced that effective June 27, it will be included in the Russell 3000, Russell 2000, and Russell Microcap indexes.

Sailfish Royalty (OTCQX:SROYF) reported Q1 revenues of $0.6 million, operating cash flow of -$0.2 million, and net income of -$0.1 million. Sailfish held cash of $2.2 million and debt of $4.1 million as of the end of Q1.

The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.0125, payable on July 15, to shareholders of record as of June 30. In the same news release, Vox announced the conversion of outstanding convertible debentures into 4,258,335 new shares.

On May 14, Sailfish announced that Solidus Resources received a Letter of Interest from the Export-Import Bank of the United States regarding financing of up to $835 million of the Spring Valley gold project CAPEX. Sailfish holds a 3% NSR royalty over the majority of the proposed Spring Valley open pit, as well as 1.5% NSR royalty and 0.5% NSR royalty over some boundary areas.

On May 27, Sailfish reported that Mako Mining (OTCQX:MAKOF) reported initial drill results from its drill campaign in the El Golfo area, where Sailfish holds a 2% NSR royalty. The best results include 117.98 g/t gold and 37 g/t silver over 2 meters, and 18.14 g/t gold and 9.5 g/t silver over 7 meters.

Elemental Altus Royalties (OTCQX:ELEMF) reported Q1 revenues of $11.6 million, operating cash flow of $2.4 million, and net income of $3.4 million. The revenues grew by more than 100% compared to the previous quarter, not only due to the higher gold prices but also due to the maiden $6.6 million revenue from the Korali-Sud royalty. Elemental reiterated its 2025 guidance of 11,600-13,200 toz of gold equivalent and revenue guidance of $30.1-34.3 million. Elemental ended Q1 with cash of $4.8 million and debt-free.

On May 1, Elemental announced that it received further $9.6 million as a result of its secured creditor claim against Rambler Metals and Mining (OTC:RBMTF).

On May 28, Elemental announced that Focus Minerals (OTCPK:FCSUF) sold its Laverton project to Genesis Minerals (OTCPK:GSISF) for A$250 million ($163 million). Laverton plans to use ore from Laverton to feed its nearby 3 Mtpa mill, which should help to speed up the development of the project. Elemental holds a 2% Gross Revenue Royalty over a significant portion of the Laverton project.

Empress Royalty (EMPYF) reported Q1 revenues of $2.7 million, operating cash flow of $1.7 million, and net income of $0.4 million. While the revenue and net income remained in line with Q4, the operating cash flow increased by 55%. As of the end of Q1, Empress held cash of $1.8 million, and debt of $4.6 million.

Star Royalties (STRFF) recorded Q1 revenues of $0.2 million, operating cash flow of -$0.4 million, and net income of -$0.3 million. As of the end of Q1, Star Royalties held cash of $3.4 million and no debt. The results of the company should improve next year, when the Copperstone gold mine (Star holds a 4% gold stream) should get into production.

Silver Crown Royalties (SCRI:CA) reported Q1 revenues of $0.3 million, operating cash flow of -$0.3 million, and net income of -$0.4 million. Similar to Star, Silver Crown's numbers remain unimpressive. Silver Crown also announced a C$2 million ($1.46 million) non-brokered private placement. The company intends to issue 307,692 units at a price of C$6.5 ($4.75) per unit. Each unit consists of one common share and one share purchase warrant, with a 3-year maturity and C$13 ($9.5) exercise price.

The June Outlook

Over the first decade of June, gold reapproached its all-time highs while silver grew to the highest value since 2012. The broader stock market is slightly up too. However, positive price action could be seen only at the smaller precious metals R&S companies. The biggest ones have been more or less flat so far. It is to be seen what the rest of the month brings; however, the growing tensions in the Middle East could push the gold price to new record highs, which should be positive for the precious metals R&S companies.

data.title: Jane's April 2025 Retirement Income Update: A Busy Month For Trading
data.description:
April represented one of the biggest months for trades in Jane's retirement accounts, and it's no surprise given the market volatility that we saw after the announcement of sweeping tariffs. Since this time, the market has rebounded strongly, and we no longer see the same kind of discounts for high-quality companies.

The most notable buy that, I think, is worth discussing comes from LyondellBasell (LYB) which recently announced a 2.2% dividend increase even as many analysts cited concerns of the potential dividend cut as the company has placed its focus on restructuring the company with a combination of capital reductions and exiting ventures like the refining business that it does not consider to be core to the business model.

While a dividend increase does not in and of itself signal that the dividend is safe, it is traditionally regarded as a strong sign that management is optimistic about the direction.

The question after the recent earnings rebound is whether or not this represents a true turning point for LYB or if the perceived challenges from the chemical industry will create further issues for the stock price. It is worth noting that the company has continued to reduce its share count, which is an activity that would traditionally be avoided if they were put in a financial position where they could not afford it.

LYB currently has just shy of $2 billion cash on hand and based on the stable debt levels since the end of the pandemic we can also say that they do not appear to be relying too heavily on debt during this restructuring.

LYB's situation appears to be the kind of rare situation where the market has misunderstood what is taking place with its restructuring, and this has only been fueled by concerns about tariffs. It's possible that it's coincidence, but on the same day that we receive word that the U.S. and China have agreed to a trade framework we also saw LYB's stock price jump from its lowest level at the beginning of June to its highest point since the stock price dropped off as a result of tariff announcements.

Ironically, LYB is a very international company that has the ability to circumvent many of the tariffs or at least reduce the impact by producing materials in the country that they are used in. I think this is a great example of noise that makes investors believe the situation is even more complicated than it really is and is resulting in downward pressure on the stock price.

We have a full position in LYB which is why we haven't added even more shares, but still see the current price as a great entry point for those who are willing to tolerate the volatility we are likely to see over the next few quarters.

Dividend Increases

There were a total of four companies in the Traditional or Roth IRAs that paid a special dividend or increased dividends during the month of April.

  • Americold Realty (COLD) - Dividend increased by 4.5%.
  • Iron Mountain (IRM) - Dividend increased by 9.8%.
  • Realty Income (O) - Dividend increased by .2%.
  • W. P. Carey (WPC) - Dividend increased by 1.1%.

April Trades - Traditional IRA

There were several trades executed in the month of April in Jane's Traditional IRA. All trades initiated were increased to already existing positions.

We had some really great entry points in the trades above, with a few premature purchases that could have benefitted from waiting even if it meant waiting just a few days. We spend very little time thinking about the situations where we could have exhibited more patience because as long as we believe the entry point seems appropriate, Jane will continue to collect dividend income as we wait for share prices to recover.

Avient (AVNT) presented a buying opportunity, one of which was triggered by a limit purchase that had been in places previous to the tariff announcement. After the tariff announcement, we also took the opportunity to add some of the lowest cost shares that we maintain in the AVNT position.

Blue Owl Capital (OBDC) shares over the last year have not been available at the $14.00/share price that we locked in. Jane has a substantial position in Main Street Capital (MAIN) but the reality is that shares are trading at a substantial premium which makes OBDC a much more attractive option at this point in time.

Archer-Daniels-Midland (ADM) is trading at five-year lows and even though dividend growth has slowed, the entry point provides a 4.5% dividend yield with share appreciation upside.

BlackRock Health Sciences Trust (BME) was traded at lows that haven't been seen since 2020. BME is currently the only exposure that Jane maintains when it comes to the pharmaceutical industry. I prefer to rely on experts with a better understanding of the industry due to the complexity that comes from FDA testing and approval/denial.

April Trades - Roth IRA

There was a total of one trade made in the Roth IRA during the month of April.

COLD recently announced its dividend increase, and the share price has reached levels that haven't been seen since the stock went public back in 2017. We have since added additional shares at an even more attractive price point, with the stock price being cut nearly in half in the last twelve months.

Portfolio Composition

The images below are focused on what is happening now and moving forward.

The first image shows what has happened year-over-year with the portfolio in terms of what holdings are generating income. I made the change part way through 2024 to highlight the companies where the payout increased as a result of a special dividend or dividend increase. This is designated by the color orange.

Below is an updated table of the extremely conservative forecast that suggests estimated income will be down -8.9% in the Traditional IRA (previously -10.1% in March) and up .9% in the Roth IRA (previously .5% in March) for FY-2025.

I have added the third table to show the combined numbers and created a new column to give a better perspective on the monthly change when it comes to year-over-year income, which is expected to be down -5.7% overall (previously -6.6% in March).

The next two images have been updated from my forecasting articles to look at how much the income has grown on a monthly basis and separate out what income is from dividends and what comes from CDs/Money Markets, etc.

This offers more insight/context as to where Jane's income is being derived (equities, fixed income, etc.). These are also more realistic numbers of what we expect to see for income growth, and also give us the ability to better track which months have the largest impact.

Here is a basic understanding of what each of the columns/fields means:

  • 2024 - Income results for 2024
  • 2025 (Ex CDs) - Estimated income 2025 (yellow) and actual income 2025 (green) but excludes all income earned from money markets, CDs, etc.
  • Special Dividend - Provides context for irregular or one-time cash-flows that distort the income number.
  • 2025 (W CDs) - Estimated CD & money market income 2025 (yellow) and actual income 2025 (green). Separated because we want to differentiate between dividend income and growth compared to CDs/money markets that are not subject to increases.
  • Total Income - Combined total income from dividends, CDs, Money market, etc.
  • 3%/5%/7% Increase - These columns serve as a marker so you can see how much income would need to be hit that month to achieve a specific increase. I will likely change these numbers to be more account-specific in the future (some accounts are growing more rapidly than others).

It is worth nothing that the original forecast tables reflected an expected reduction of income at -14.0% and -.9% at the estimates phase (before the collection of actual income numbers). This shows that income is trending in a positive direction (as expected) but will be subject to the loss of income due to the expiration of certificates of deposit (primarily in the Traditional IRA).

Conclusion

Although income growth was flat in the Traditional IRA and income actually reduced in the Roth IRA, we still saw the estimated FY-2025 income deficiency improve. This improvement comes from the fact that the actual amounts received were greater than the original estimates used.

Originally, we estimated that the Traditional IRA would generate $1,587.93 of income during the month of April, but the actual amount received was $1,867.64.

The Roth IRA was estimated to generate $832.78 of dividend income, but ultimately generated $879.27 of dividend income.

Account balances appear to have found their footing, with the trend looking strong for the average balance in FY-2025 in both accounts.

Finding value in shares where we don't currently have a full position appears to be the biggest challenge. With the certificates of deposit expiring, we are looking to redeploy funds but want to make sure that we aren't buying shares at peak value and locking in sub-standard returns with a lower yield as a result of this.

What stocks are you seeing that aren't in Jane's Retirement portfolio? Are there any compelling investments I should be looking at? I would love to hear any ideas/suggestions in the comment section.

John and Jane are long all holdings mentioned in this article.

data.title: Circle IPO: The Perfect Timing
data.description:
As a long-term BTC and ETH investor, Circle's (NYSE:CRCL) IPO caught my attention recently. I've known stablecoins like USDC and USDT for many years. As much as I'm excited to see Circle's stock rally over the past few days, I was more surprised by how profitable stablecoins can be and how Circle has evolved beyond just USDC. After reading its IPO prospectus, I realize that the company is not just a stable coin, and it may have the potential to transform into a strong global payment network.

For those unfamiliar with USDC and digital currency, here's some quick background on Circle.

Circle launched USDC in 2018 as a stablecoin to act as a medium for moving funds between different cryptocurrencies without needing to convert back to fiat currency (USD). All cryptocurrencies need trading pairs, similar to real currency. If you want to move from one cryptocurrency to another by converting to USD each time, it's costly and time-consuming. Stablecoins are pegged to USD at 1:1 ratio, so you can view them as digital USD. It's like chips in a casino. You give $100 to the cashier for $100 in chips, play in the casino, and redeem the chips when you're done. The casino guarantees the chips' value is 1:1 to real dollars.

Also, due to the volatility of most coins, sometimes stablecoins act as a base for many decentralized finance applications, or what we call DeFi. Stablescoins are essential for staking (earning yields), lending and providing liquidity for different trading pairs.

According to Circle's prospectus, USDC is the second-largest stablecoin with a 24% share of the stablecoin market as of December 31, 2024. The largest stablecoin is USDT, issued by Tether. According to CoinMarketCap, Circle has currently issued $61.55B in USDC while Tether has issued $155.5B in USDT, more than twice the volume, and takes around 60% of market shares.

Trust And Transparency

Though USDC is smaller in volume, it's the preferred stablecoin for many large institutions due to its transparency. The company states: "All Circle stablecoins minted and outstanding are backed by at least an equivalent amount of fiat currency-denominated reserve assets held in accounts." All stablecoin companies claim they're safe and backed by real fiat assets, but some have proven otherwise, like the famous death spiral of Terra Luna's stablecoin. Even with USDT, some analysts claim an unexpected EU ban or liquidity crisis could cause USDT to collapse. Trust and liquidity are crucial for stablecoins, just like traditional banking. If most clients lost trust in the bank and decided to withdraw deposits at the same time, the bank would collapse. Therefore, I believe Circle being the first public company with a transparent balance sheet is very beneficial, and it might motivate people to move from USDT to USDC in the long run. Remember, USDT has 60% of the market share. If 5% of USDT users move to USDC, it can boost Circle's market value by another $7- $8 billions.

Perfect Timing

The timing of this IPO couldn't be better. On June 17, the US Senate approved the Guiding and Establishing National Innovation for US Stablecoins (GENIUS) Act by a solid majority of 68 to 30 vote. The GENIUS Act requires "every payment stablecoin to hold reserves equal to the number of tokens in circulation, limited to short-dated US Treasuries or insured deposits, and bars issuers from paying yield." This is exactly what Circle says it's doing. This law signals the government's endorsement of digital currency, which is a big confidence boost for investors. Once the GENIUS Act categorizes stablecoins as payment tools, similar to credit cards or bank accounts, this officially moves USDC from crypto-only finance to traditional finance. The market opportunities are limitless.

Since the law passed, shares of Circle have jumped significantly and many governments in Asia have been discussing whether the government shall launch official stable coins. It is a strong signal that stable coins like USDC are now officially becoming mainstream.

Speaking of challenging traditional finance, Circle's payment network has worked with Visa and Mastercard, and this is where things get interesting. When people send money internationally through big banks, it takes days and costs $25-50 in fees. Globally, banks use a secure messaging system called SWIFT to communicate and to authenticate transactions. Circle could potentially challenge SWIFT. It can build a similar secured communication system between different financial institutions and lower the transaction cost and shorten the transfer time. SWIFT is built on old technology that was invented decades ago, and it was not built for speed. Circle can potentially challenge that.

Investment banks like BlackRock and BNY Mellon have already partnered with Circle. According to its latest press release, Intercontinental Exchange Inc. (ICE) also signed an agreement with Circle recently to “explore using Circle's stablecoin USDC, as well as their tokenized money market offering US Yield Coin (USYC), to develop new products and solutions for customers”.

According to Grand View Research, the global cross-border payments industry is around $212.55 billion in 2024, projected to grow at 7.1% CAGR through 2030. If Circle could capture 2-5% of the cross-border payments market as it described in the prospectus, that can translate into $4-11B in annual transaction volume potential by 2030.

I believe Circle has significant first-mover advantages in this space. Building up a 1:1 USD pegged digital currency sounds easy, but setting up API integrations and cross-border onchain and offchain payment infrastructure takes considerable time and resources. The company's existing partnerships with major financial institutions, exchanges, and payment processors create switching costs and network effects that will be difficult for competitors to replicate quickly.

Strong Financials

Q1 revenue reached $578 million, a 58.5% increase YoY, with net income of $64.8 million vs. $48.6 million in the prior year, a 33.2% increase. Both topline and bottom-line show strong growth momentum. USDC transaction volume surged, with roughly $6 trillion processed in Q1 and over $25 trillion since launch.

Taking a deeper dive into the P&L, we can see the company's main expenses are distribution and transaction costs, which account for almost 40% of total revenue. According to the prospectus, Circle shares revenue “generated from USDC reserves pro rata based on the amount of USDC distributed and held on each respective platform”. Among all platforms, Coinbase (Nasdaq: COIN) is the biggest winner. According to the prospectus, "For the years ended December 31, 2024, 2023, and 2022, we incurred $907.9 million, $691.3 million, and $248.1 million, respectively, of distribution costs in connection with our agreements with Coinbase." To put this in perspective, Circle's total 2024 revenue was only $1.66 billion, but they paid $907.9 million to Coinbase, more than half their total revenue.

If Circle grows larger and gains stronger bargaining power to renegotiate its revenue share percentage with different platforms, especially Coinbase, the company's margins could be significantly higher.

Potential Risks

The two biggest risks for Circle are falling yields on short-term US bonds and rising competition.

Regarding interest rate risk, Circle's revenue model is highly sensitive to Federal Reserve rate changes. Here's how different scenarios impact Circle's revenue assuming $43.9B USDC outstanding as of December 31, 2024:

Fed Funds Rate

Annual Revenue Impact

Net Margin Est.

Revenue Change

2.0%

4.0%

$1.76B

9.2%

+30%

6.0%
